channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🔌 NATO Launches First Response to Suspected Deep-Sea Cable Sabotage
item.description: A suspected sabotage attack on a deep-sea communications cable has triggered NATO's first coordinated response under its new critical infrastructure protection framework, marking a significant escalation in hybrid warfare tactics.

item.title: 🇨🇩 Rwanda-Backed Rebels Seize Congo's Humanitarian Hub City
item.description: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels have entered a key safe-haven city in eastern DR Congo that serves as a critical aid hub, raising fears for hundreds of thousands of displaced civilians and threatening regional stability.

item.title: ✈️ Italy Backs Saudi Arabia Joining UK-Japan Fighter Jet Program
item.description: Italian PM Meloni announced support for Saudi Arabia's participation in the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), signaling deepening defense ties between Gulf states and Western allies amid shifting regional dynamics.

item.title: 🌍 Extreme Weather Events Threaten Global Democracy, Study Warns
item.description: A new report finds that heatwaves, floods, and wildfires are increasingly threatening democratic institutions worldwide, disrupting elections and destabilizing governments in climate-vulnerable nations.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo Skips Major African Catholic Nations on Continental Tour
item.description: Pope Leo's Africa tour is drawing attention for skipping several of the continent's largest Catholic populations, raising questions about the Vatican's strategic priorities in the region.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Ramps Up Ship Attacks in Hormuz After Trump Ceasefire Extension
item.description: Iran has intensified attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz despite the US ceasefire extension, raising doubts about the durability of any peace agreement. The attacks come as Tehran refuses to commit to substantive negotiations.

item.title: ✈️ USAF Doubles F-15EX Eagle II Order as Fleet Modernization Accelerates
item.description: The US Air Force has doubled its procurement order for the F-15EX Eagle II fighter jet, providing a ready successor to aging F-15E Strike Eagles. The decision reflects growing defense needs amid global tensions.

item.title: 💣 Iranian Mines in Hormuz Could Take Six Months to Clear
item.description: Iranian naval mines deployed in the Strait of Hormuz could require up to six months of clearing operations, according to reports, prolonging the disruption to global oil shipments even if hostilities cease.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel's Dependence on Chinese Drones Exposes IDF Vulnerabilities
item.description: Israel's military reliance on Chinese-made drones has created strategic vulnerabilities, as the IDF's supply chain depends on a country with close ties to Iran. The dependence raises concerns about technology transfers and operational security.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Iran War Has Depleted Half of US Patriot and THAAD Missile Stockpiles
item.description: The conflict with Iran has consumed approximately half of America's Patriot and THAAD missile defense interceptors, raising concerns about US military readiness for other potential conflicts.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China builds new island outpost in disputed South China Sea waters near Vietnam
item.description: China has been quietly constructing an artificial island in disputed waters off Vietnam's coast, expanding its strategic footprint in the South China Sea amid ongoing territorial disputes with neighbors.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇶 US halts dollar shipments to Iraq in effort to sever Iran ties
item.description: The United States has stopped dollar shipments to Iraq's cash-based economy, using Baghdad's own oil revenues as leverage to force the Iraqi government to distance itself from Iran amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇦 Russia forces Ukrainians in occupied territories to accept Russian property titles or face eviction
item.description: A new Russian law requires Ukrainians in captured regions to obtain Russian title deeds or risk losing their homes, darkening Moscow's portrayal of normalcy in occupied territories.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇲🇽 Two CIA officers killed in Mexico counter-cartel operation identified
item.description: The two Americans killed in a vehicle crash in Chihuahua state were CIA officers returning from a joint counter-cartel operation with Mexican armed forces, raising questions about US involvement in Mexico's drug war.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Millions of Indians stripped of voting rights before critical West Bengal election
item.description: Muslims and other minorities have been disproportionately deleted from electoral rolls ahead of West Bengal state elections, in what experts describe as a government effort to "purify" voter lists.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky Pushes to Restart Peace Talks, Open to Any Venue Except Russia, Belarus
item.description: President Zelensky said Ukraine is ready to meet with Russian representatives in any country except Russia or Belarus, calling for resumption of U.S.-mediated talks. The appeal comes as peace negotiations have remained frozen for nearly two months, with Washington shifting focus to the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Approves 20th Russia Sanctions Package After Hungary, Slovakia Drop Veto
item.description: EU ambassadors agreed on the 20th package of sanctions against Moscow after Hungary and Slovakia lifted their veto. The move follows Orban election defeat and paves way for a 90-billion-euro loan for Kyiv. Ukraine also resumed oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline.

item.title: 🇲🇩 Moldova Oligarch Plahotniuc Sentenced to 19 Years in Prison
item.description: Vladimir Plahotniuc, once Moldova most powerful oligarch who de facto controlled the country government in the 2010s, was sentenced to 19 years for embezzlement, fraud and money laundering in connection with the 2014 theft of billion from three Moldovan banks. His fall marks Moldova alignment with European democratic values.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Praises African Nations for Blocking Taiwan President Flight
item.description: China expressed "high appreciation" for Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar after they denied overflight rights to Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te plane, forcing cancellation of his trip to Eswatini. Beijing denied applying economic pressure while praising the countries for supporting the one-China principle.

item.title: ⚔️ US Seizes Iranian-Linked Ship in Indian Ocean, Extends Ceasefire Deadline
item.description: U.S. forces boarded the sanctioned oil tanker M/T Tifani in the Indian Ocean, marking the first interdiction outside the Middle East since Operation Epic Fury began. Trump announced an extended ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan request while maintaining the blockade, as Iranian officials rejected the terms.

item.title: 🚢⚠️ Iran Fires on Three Ships in Strait of Hormuz as US Blockade Continues
item.description: Iranian forces targeted three vessels in the strategic waterway, including one that was heavily damaged, as Tehran escalates pressure following the US naval blockade and seized cargo ship.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU Approves €90 Billion Loan for Ukraine as Druzhba Pipeline Restarts
item.description: The massive loan package comes as Ukraine reopens the Druzhba oil pipeline, ending a months-long stalemate over stalled supplies to Hungary and signaling continued Western support.

item.title: 🛢️🇷🇺 Russia to Halt Oil Flow to Germany via Druzhba Pipeline from May 1
item.description: Moscow confirmed it will suspend shipments of Kazakh oil to Germany through the pipeline, adding another layer of energy disruption to European markets already strained by the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan President Cancels Trip After African Countries Revoke Flight Permits
item.description: Taiwan accused Beijing of pressuring African nations to close their airspace to President Lai Ching-te’s plane, the latest in a series of Chinese efforts to isolate Taiwan diplomatically.

item.title: 🇳🇬⚖️ Nigerian Coup Plot Suspects Deny Treason Charges in Court
item.description: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled, marking the latest political turmoil in Africa’s most populous nation.
